chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الصَّدَقَةِ لِبَنِي هَاشِمٍ وَ مَوَالِيهِمْ وَ صِلَتِهِمْ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 89 – The charity to the Clan of Hashim and their slaves and their connected ones

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: كالموثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/191)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: كالموثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/191)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الصَّدَقَةِ الَّتِي حُرِّمَتْ عَلَى بَنِي هَاشِمٍ مَا هِيَ قَالَ هِيَ الزَّكَاةُ قُلْتُ فَتَحِلُّ صَدَقَةُ بَعْضِهِمْ عَلَى بَعْضٍ قَالَ نَعَمْ.

en.hubeali: Humeyd Bin Ziyad, from Ibn Sama’at, from someone else, from Aban Bin Usman, from Ismail Bin Al Fazl Al Hashimy who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about the charity which is prohibited unto the Clan of Hashim<sup>as</sup>, what is it?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘It is the Zakat’. I said, ‘So is the charity Permissible upon each other?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes’.
en.sarwar: 5. Hamid ibn Ziyad has narrated from (ibn) Sama‘ah, from more than one person from Aban ibn ‘Uthman from Isma‘il ibn al-Fadl al- Hashimiy who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about charity which it is not lawful for children of al- Hashim to receive. The Imam replied, ‘It is zakat.’ I then asked, ‘Can they receive zakat from each other?’ He replied, ‘Yes, they can do so. ’’’(This Hadith is best explained by Fatwa).
